decline_history.reason: Bio sourcing: unfamiliar referencing style (WP:REFB); YouTube and Vimeo not reliable sources

open_reviewer_notes.note: In 20 years of editing here I have not come across this referencing style and YouTube and Vimeo are not considered reliable sources
Notability does not hinge on the existence of the subjects work. Need media coverage about him in newspapers, magazines, books. YouTube, Vimeo, self-published websites, blogs not acceptable.

targets.title: Amos Le Blanc (film director)
targets.notes: Draft exists at Draft:Amos Le Blanc but account is blocked. Clean revision prepared (9,996 bytes, zero YouTube/Vimeo, standard ref tags) but cannot be posted. Needs human editor with no COI.

assessed_2026-05-25.assessment: JPEGMAFIA already has a Wikipedia article. No new article needed. Album article for EXPERIMENTAL RAP could be a candidate but requires third-party sourcing from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, NME, etc. Not actionable with blocked account.

assessed_2026-05-25.article: Bleachers and everyone for ten minutes
assessed_2026-05-25.assessment: Bleachers and Jack Antonoff both have Wikipedia articles. Album article for everyone for ten minutes may be warranted given commercial and critical profile but requires third-party sourcing and an unblocked account.

assessed_2026-05-26.article: Aja Monet and the color of rain
assessed_2026-05-26.assessment: Aja Monet has an existing Wikipedia article. Her new album the color of rain (released May 22, 2026 on Drink Sum Wtr) does not yet have a Wikipedia album article. The artonly piece references AllMusic (score 90), Carnegie Hall performance, and notable collaborators (Meshell Ndegeocello, Ambrose Akinmusire, Brandee Younger, Mick Jenkins, Vic Mensa, Ganavya). The album is too recent (4 days old) to have sufficient third-party coverage to establish notability. Monitor for Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, NPR Music, Guardian coverage. Not actionable now: account blocked and album too new.

assessed_2026-05-26.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Ed O'Brien pageid=273486; EOB (musician) pageid=63179030
assessed_2026-05-26.assessment: Ed O'Brien has an existing Wikipedia article. EOB (musician) has an existing Wikipedia article. Blue Morpho (album) already has a Wikipedia article. No new article needed.

assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: MISSING (Traumprinz, Phillip Lauer, Tuff City Kids all absent from English Wikipedia)
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Phillip Lauer (Traumprinz) is a German electronic producer from the Cologne scene, also known as Prince of Denmark and one half of Tuff City Kids with Gerd Janson. Life is the fifth Traumprinz album on the All Possible Worlds imprint. No English Wikipedia articles exist for Traumprinz, Phillip Lauer, or Tuff City Kids. The artist has significant underground credibility but primarily exists within specialist electronic music press (Resident Advisor, Wire, etc.) rather than mainstream RS. A Traumprinz or Phillip Lauer Wikipedia article would require sourcing from Resident Advisor, Groove Magazine, The Wire, FACT Mag, or similar RS that provide substantial coverage. Not actionable: account blocked and notability would need verification against WP:GNG via RS search.

assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: EXISTS Lowertown (band) pageid=72035125 (last touched 2026-05-23, 6848 bytes). Article covers through debut album I Love to Lie (2022). Does not mention Ugly Duckling Union.
assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_album_article: MISSING (Ugly Duckling Union has no Wikipedia article)
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Lowertown (band) has an existing Wikipedia article. Their second album Ugly Duckling Union (May 2026, Summer Shade / Run For Cover Records) received Stereogum Album of the Week and NPR Music top 5 of its release week. The band article is outdated and does not mention the new album. An Ugly Duckling Union album article would be supportable given Stereogum and NPR Music coverage, but account is blocked. The band article itself could be updated with the new album when an unblocked editor is available.
assessed_2026-05-28.note: Lowertown band article (pageid 72035125) needs updating to include Ugly Duckling Union. Album article creation viable if more RS coverage accumulates.

assessed_2026-05-28.article: Mexican Institute of Sound and Meridian Brothers / Ruido Tovar
assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_artist_article: Mexican Institute of Sound EXISTS pageid=13468391. Meridian Brothers EXISTS pageid=76600887. Rigo Tovar (historical figure referenced in title) EXISTS pageid=1668428.
assessed_2026-05-28.wikipedia_album_article: MISSING (Ruido Tovar album has no Wikipedia article)
assessed_2026-05-28.assessment: Both collaborating artists have Wikipedia articles. The album Ruido Tovar is a 2026 release on Ansonia Records featuring a guest appearance by Beck. No Wikipedia album article exists. Given the profile of the collaborators (Camilo Lara / MIS and Eblis Alvarez / Meridian Brothers are both notable enough for existing Wikipedia articles) and the Beck feature, an album article may be viable if third-party RS coverage from Pitchfork, Bandcamp Daily, Resident Advisor, The Wire, or NPR Music materializes. Not actionable: account blocked.
